summary: Excellent soundboard recording of an iconic 8‑21‑72 Berkeley show, highlighted by a monster Dark Star→El Paso→Space→Morning Dew sequence and stellar playing throughout.
review: The SBD capture is crisp, album‑quality and virtually free of hiss, letting the band’s 1972 peak shine through.  The performance is consistently high‑energy – the first set rocks with Europe‑72 vibes while the second set delivers one of the most revered Dark Star journeys ever, plus standout renditions of Playing in the Band, China Cat > Rider and Stella Blue.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars, praising both musicianship (especially Dark Star/El Paso) and recording fidelity.  The balance of a superb musical performance (≈70% weight) with an excellent soundboard source (≈30% weight) yields a rating in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix, strong piano presence, occasional vintage tape hiss noted but not detrimental; praised as “album‑quality” and “top‑notch”.

standout_songs: Dark Star > El Paso > Space > Morning Dew
poor_songs: Dark Star (one reviewer found it too chaotic – 2‑star rating)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set mirrors the tight Europe‑72 style with strong tunes like Sugaree, Jack Straw and The Other One; a single‑drummer groove gives extra clarity.  The second set erupts into an epic Dark Star suite that flows seamlessly into El Paso, Space and Morning Dew, followed by high‑octane jams (Playing in the Band, China Cat > Rider) and a soulful close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s wah‑wah guitar work is repeatedly praised; his vocal delivery on Stella Blue and other ballads noted as powerful.  He “spins scorching licks” during Dark Star and leads the jam with intensity.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass harmony shines, especially on Jack Straw and in backing vocals; his “bombs” are highlighted in China Cat > Rider and he often drives the groove when the band expands into space jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal clarity is lauded (especially on Jack Straw) and his energy throughout the set earns frequent compliments.  His rhythm guitar support is described as solid and enthusiastic.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano work receives consistent acclaim – “sparkling riffs”, “jazzy thrust” that fuels the Dark Star/El Paso sequence and adds depth to many cuts (e.g., FOD, China Cat).
band_member_comments.Drums: The post‑Pigpen single‑drummer configuration (Bill Kreutzmann) is noted for providing a tighter, more focused rhythm section.
